Title: TaWUS-like-5D affects grain weight and filling by inhibiting the expression of sucrose and trehalose metabolism-related genes in wheat grain endosperm

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Interacts with TaWUS-like-5D and is implicated in regulating sugar metabolism, ABA interaction, and starch synthesis in developing wheat grains.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TaSnRK1-b1-4B physically interacts with TaWUS-like-5D in yeast, luciferase complementation, and BiFC assays. 2. The interaction links TaWUS-like-5D to the T6P-SnRK1 signaling pathway controlling sucrose utilization and starch synthesis. 3. The paper proposes that TaSnRK1-b1-4B participates in sugar/ABA regulatory crosstalk affecting grain filling.
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: SNF1-related protein kinase 1 regulatory beta subunit
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y2H; luciferase complementation imaging assay; BiFC
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: developing grains at 10 DPA under field growth conditions
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: major storage carbohydrate supporting grain filling and kernel weight
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of developing grains at 10 DPA; no accession reported
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential component of the T6P-SnRK1 module to consider when engineering grain filling and starch deposition, but not directly manipulated for breeding in this study.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study did not genetically manipulate TaSnRK1-b1-4B itself; its role is inferred from physical interaction and pathway context rather than direct mutant or overexpression phenotyping.

Regulation_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: Chinese Spring reference; haplotypes analyzed in Chinese wheat germplasm
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Kenong 199 (KN199); Kronos; Chinese Spring; Shi4185; Zhongyou9507; Hongjinmai
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Negative regulator of grain filling, starch accumulation, thousand kernel weight, and yield; overexpression decreases starch, amylose, amylopectin, grain size and yield, whereas RNAi or knockout increases kernel size/TKW and can increase yield.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TaWUS-like-5D was mapped as the major TKW-associated gene on chromosome 5DS and is highly expressed in developing grains, especially at 10 DPA. 2. Overexpression reduced grain size, TKW, starch, amylose, amylopectin, and yield, while RNAi and knockout mutants increased grain size and TKW, with RNAi lines showing about 10.0% yield increase. 3. TaWUS-like-5D represses sucrose metabolism-, trehalose/T6P metabolism-, starch synthesis-, and some hormone-related genes in developing grains, leading to impaired sucrose utilization and reduced starch synthesis. 4. TaWUS-like-5D physically interacts with TaSnRK1-b1-4B, linking it to the T6P-SnRK1-ABA regulatory module.
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: WUSCHEL-related homeobox (WOX/WUS clade) transcription factor; homeobox domain; WUS box
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional repression/inhibition of downstream metabolic genes; protein interaction with SnRK1 beta subunit
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Inhibits expression of SUS-2A/2B/2D/7B/7D/3B; SS-7A/7B/7D; SBE-2A; CesA-1D/2A/3B/5B/5D/6A; CeL-3B; TPS-1A/5D/6B; TPS6-1A/3A/3B/3D; TPP-6D; decreases IPT-1D/5A/5D, ARF-3B, TCP-5B; increases GA3ox-2A, NCED-5B, TaNAC019-A in OE grains
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: SUS genes; TPS/TPS6; TPP; SS genes; SBE-2A; CesA/CeL genes; IPT genes; GA3ox-2A; NCED-5B
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: sucrose lysis and utilization; trehalose-6-phosphate signaling; starch biosynthesis; cellulose/lignin balance; hormone-mediated grain filling
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major negative regulator of grain endosperm starch accumulation and thousand kernel weight
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Proposed involvement in the T6P-SnRK1-ABA module; possible feedback via SnRK1 activity was discussed but not fully established
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Physically interacts with TaSnRK1-b1-4B, a regulatory beta subunit of the heterotrimeric SnRK1 complex
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Preferentially expressed in young spikes and developing grains; highest at 10 DPA; lower in high-TKW varieties than low-TKW varieties during 5-10 DPA
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Preferentially expressed in young spikes and developing grains; peak at 10 DPA
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Overexpression driven by pWMB110 vector promoter; RNAi hairpin in pAHC-PSK; specific promoter type not stated
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: grain development/filling stage, especially 10 DPA; field conditions in Beijing across multiple seasons
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: major storage carbohydrate determining grain filling, sink strength, kernel weight, and yield
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression caused dwarfism, reduced plant height, fewer grains and tillers, wider tiller angle, impaired grain filling, lower starch, and ~48.3% lower yield; complete triple knockout caused inadequate grain filling and reduced starch despite longer grains, suggesting dosage balance among homoeologs is important
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS; BSA; BSR-seq; haplotype association; qRT-PCR; overexpression; RNAi; CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; mutant analysis in Kronos; starch/sugar/hormone phenotyping; RNA-seq; Y2H; luciferase complementation; BiFC
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: GWAS; BSA-seq; BSR-seq; RNA-seq of 10 DPA grains on Illumina HiSeq 2500; no public accession reported
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for CRISPR knockout or downregulation to improve wheat TKW, grain filling, starch accumulation, and yield; dosage and homoeolog balance should be considered because complete triple knockout caused inadequate filling
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study distinguishes homoeolog dosage effects: single/double loss-of-function can improve TKW, but triple knockout (aabbdd) reduced starch and caused inadequate filling, indicating subfunctionalization and need for balanced TaWUS-like dosage.
